concept_name: Potential of a System of Charges — V = Σ k qᵢ/rᵢ (a scalar sum of signed numbers)
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.2 Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance (§2.5 Potential due to a system of charges) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (drop a probe near three fixed charges and write each charge's own potential k q_i / r_i → recognise the total V as a plain SCALAR sum of the three signed numbers, no arrows → push the probe far so one charge is distant and confront the 'only the nearest charge matters' belief: the far term shrinks but never zeroes → slide the probe onto the centre line equidistant from an equal +q and −q and confront the 'two nearby charges must give a big V' belief: the pair cancels exactly to zero → then the superpower contrast: the same charges' FIELD needs hard vector addition by components, while V was one easy signed sum → hand the explorer the draggable probe + a q3 slider), never copied. The atomic idea: the potential of a system of point charges at a field point is V = Σ k q_i / r_i — the algebraic SUM of each charge's own potential, where r_i is that charge's distance to the point. Because potential is a SCALAR, you add signed NUMBERS directly (no directions, no components, no angles). Every charge contributes a nonzero k q_i / r_i term; distance only shrinks a term, it never zeroes it. An equal +q and −q equidistant from the point cancel exactly (+kq/r + (−kq/r) = 0). Signs and distances set the total, not the count of charges. The headline contrast (the aha): the SAME charges' electric FIELD needs vector addition by components, while the potential is a single easy scalar sum — that is why potential is the friendlier road. SCOPE: this is the system-of-charges POTENTIAL diamond of Ch.2 — it generalises the single-charge V = kQ/r and the two-charge dipole sum to N charges via superposition. It declares electric_potential_point_charge (V = kQ/r), electric_potential_dipole (the two-charge sum) and electric_potential_meaning (V = W/q) as prerequisites and does NOT re-teach them. It does NOT cover continuous charge distributions, the field E (vector) of a system, capacitance, or potential energy of a system. NEW field_3d scenario_type system_of_charges: three fixed charges (+q₁, −q₂, +2q₃) + a probe with per-charge potential tags, a running signed-sum panel, a far-charge term highlight, an equal-pair cancellation reveal, a scalar-sum-vs-vector-field contrast, and a draggable probe + q3 slider (potential_explorer). Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict→reveal).

real_world_anchor.primary: Rub three balloons on your hair and stick them to a wall — each now carries its own charge. Hold your hand at one spot near the wall. The voltage your hand feels there is not from any single balloon; it is the simple sum of what each balloon adds on its own, each contribution set by that balloon's charge and how far away it sits. A nearby balloon adds a lot, a far one adds only a little — but every one still adds something. And if one balloon were rubbed the opposite way so it pushed instead of pulled, its contribution would carry a minus sign, and an equal pull-and-push pair sitting the same distance away would cancel to nothing. The total voltage at your hand is just those signed numbers added up.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Tiny charged dust specks clinging to a screen each set up their own little voltage in the air nearby. At any point between them the voltage is the running sum of every speck's signed contribution — the close specks dominate, the far ones add a faint amount, and equal opposite specks at equal distance quietly cancel.

q3_value.name: Signed magnitude of charge q3 (S6 slider flips/scales; q1=+1, q2=-1 fixed)

DEMO_E.name: Demo field readout scale (S5 contrast only)

V_total_demo.formula: DEMO_VP * 1 / Math.max(Math.sqrt((px+1.2)*(px+1.2) + py*py), clamp_r_min) + DEMO_VP * (-1) / Math.max(Math.sqrt((px-1.2)*(px-1.2) + py*py), clamp_r_min) + DEMO_VP * q3_value / Math.max(Math.sqrt(px*px + (py-1.4)*(py-1.4)), clamp_r_min)

formulas.scalar_sum: add signed numbers directly — no directions/components/angles
formulas.field_contrast: E = vector sum by components; V = scalar sum of numbers

constraints: V is the SCALAR sum of k*q_i/r_i — one signed number per charge, added directly (C1).
Each term carries its own sign; add signed scalars, never vectors, components or angles (C2).
Every charge contributes a nonzero k*q_i/r_i term; distance shrinks a term but never zeroes it (C3).
An equal +q and -q equidistant from the point cancel exactly to 0 (C4).
Signs and distances set the total, not the count of charges (C5).
The FIELD at the same point needs vector/component addition — the scalar-sum potential is the superpower (C6).
The on-screen V uses round DEMO units (DEMO_VP = 12), not SI volts (C8).

aha_moment.statement: Potentials just add as signed numbers — the field needs hard vector addition.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: On the scalar side the three signed numbers collapse to one total, while the same charges' E arrows sprawl tip-to-tail, needing components.

STATE_1.title: Three charges + a probe — each gives its own potential k q / r

scene_composition.text: three fixed charges: +q₁, −q₂, +2q₃ — drop a probe at one point
each charge sets its own V at the probe: k q / r
read them: +3.4 from q₁, −6.7 from q₂, +11.9 from q₃

tts_sentences.text_en: Here are three fixed charges: a positive charge q₁, a negative charge q₂, and a stronger positive charge q₃. Drop a probe at one point.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ तीन fixed charges हैं: एक positive charge q₁, एक negative charge q₂, और एक stronger positive charge q₃। एक point पर probe रखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ మూడు fixed charges ఉన్నాయి: ఒక positive charge q₁, ఒక negative charge q₂, మరియు ఒక stronger positive charge q₃. ఒక point వద్ద probe పెట్టండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Each charge sets up its own potential at the probe, k q over r — its own signed number set by its charge and its distance r.
tts_sentences.text_hi: प्रत्येक charge अपना potential probe पर set करता है, k q over r — अपना signed number, जो उसके charge और उसकी distance r से तय होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి charge తన potential ని probe వద్ద set చేస్తుంది, k q over r — దాని charge మరియు దాని distance r తో నిర్ణయించబడే దాని own signed number.

tts_sentences.text_en: Read the three contributions: plus three point four from q₁, minus six point seven from q₂, plus eleven point nine from q₃.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीन contributions पढ़िए: q₁ से plus three point four, q₂ से minus six point seven, q₃ से plus eleven point nine।
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు contributions చదవండి: q₁ నుండి plus three point four, q₂ నుండి minus six point seven, q₃ నుండి plus eleven point nine.

STATE_2.title: V is a scalar — just add the three signed numbers

scene_composition.text: V is a scalar — to total it, add numbers, not arrows
predict: combine like vectors (directions), or like signed numbers?
+3.4 − 6.7 + 11.9 = +8.6 — no directions anywhere

tts_sentences.text_en: The potential V is a scalar — a plain number. So to get the total, you do not add arrows; you just add the three signed numbers.
tts_sentences.text_hi: potential V एक scalar है — एक plain number। तो total पाने के लिए आप arrows नहीं जोड़ते; बस तीन signed numbers जोड़ते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: potential V ఒక scalar — ఒక plain number. కాబట్టి total పొందడానికి మీరు arrows కలపరు; కేవలం మూడు signed numbers కలుపుతారు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict: do these combine like vectors, with directions, or like ordinary signed numbers?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Predict करिए: क्या ये vectors की तरह, directions के साथ, या ordinary signed numbers की तरह combine होते हैं?
tts_sentences.text_te: Predict చేయండి: ఇవి vectors లా, directions తో కలుస్తాయా, లేదా ordinary signed numbers లా కలుస్తాయా?

tts_sentences.text_en: Just add them: plus three point four, minus six point seven, plus eleven point nine, gives plus eight point six. No directions anywhere.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बस इन्हें जोड़िए: plus three point four, minus six point seven, plus eleven point nine, मिलता है plus eight point six। कहीं कोई directions नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: వాటిని కలపండి: plus three point four, minus six point seven, plus eleven point nine, ఇస్తుంది plus eight point six. ఎక్కడా directions లేవు.

misconception_watch.belief: To get the total potential you must combine the charges' contributions like vectors, with directions or components.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The three contributions show as plain signed numbers +3.4, −6.7, +11.9 and add straight to +8.6 — no arrows, no angles drawn anywhere.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Potential is a scalar: add the signed numbers k*q_i/r_i directly, with no directions or components.

STATE_3.title: Does the far charge still count? Every term contributes

scene_composition.text: slide the probe far right — q₁ is now over four units away
predict: only the nearest charge, or does far q₁ still count?
far q₁ still adds +2.8 — small (r is large) but never zero
total +4.2; drop the far term and it would fall to +1.4

tts_sentences.text_en: Move the probe far over to the right, so charge q₁ is now distant, more than four units away.
tts_sentences.text_hi: probe को दाईं ओर दूर ले जाइए, ताकि charge q₁ अब distant हो, चार units से ज़्यादा दूर।
tts_sentences.text_te: probe ని కుడివైపుకి దూరంగా తరలించండి, తద్వారా charge q₁ ఇప్పుడు దూరంగా ఉంటుంది, నాలుగు units కంటే ఎక్కువ దూరంలో.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict the total. Does only the nearest charge matter, or does the far charge q₁ still count in the sum?
tts_sentences.text_hi: total predict करिए। क्या केवल nearest charge मायने रखता है, या दूर का charge q₁ sum में अभी भी count करता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: total ని predict చేయండి. కేవలం nearest charge మాత్రమే important అవుతుందా, లేదా దూరపు charge q₁ sum లో ఇంకా count అవుతుందా?

tts_sentences.text_en: The far charge q₁ still adds plus two point eight — small because the distance r is large, but never zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दूर का charge q₁ अभी भी plus two point eight जोड़ता है — छोटा क्योंकि distance r बड़ी है, लेकिन कभी zero नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: దూరపు charge q₁ ఇంకా plus two point eight కలుపుతుంది — చిన్నది ఎందుకంటే distance r పెద్దది, కానీ ఎప్పటికీ zero కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Every charge contributes. The total is plus four point two; drop the far term and it would fall to plus one point four.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर charge contribute करता है। total plus four point two है; far term हटाओ तो यह plus one point four तक गिर जाएगा।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి charge contribute చేస్తుంది. total plus four point two; far term తీసివేస్తే అది plus one point four కి పడిపోతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Only the nearest charge sets the potential; distant charges contribute nothing.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The far charge q1 (r≈4.2) still adds +2.8 to the sum — dropping that term changes the total from +4.2 down to +1.4.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Every charge adds a nonzero k*q_i/r_i term; distance only shrinks it, it never makes it zero.

STATE_4.title: The equal +/− pair cancels — signs decide the sum

scene_composition.text: px = 0 exactly: the probe is equidistant from +q₁ and −q₂
two charges sit close — predict: a big V, or something surprising?
+8.9 and −8.9 cancel to exactly 0 — that pair adds nothing
total is just q₃'s +12.0 — two charges present, yet they gave nothing

tts_sentences.text_en: Now slide the probe onto the centre line, exactly equidistant from the positive charge q₁ and the negative charge q₂.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब probe को centre line पर ले जाइए, positive charge q₁ और negative charge q₂ से exactly equidistant।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు probe ని centre line పై తరలించండి, positive charge q₁ మరియు negative charge q₂ నుండి exactly equidistant.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two charges sit close by. Predict the total potential V here — a big value, or something surprising?
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो charges पास में हैं। यहाँ total potential V predict करिए — एक बड़ी value, या कुछ surprising?
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు charges దగ్గరలో ఉన్నాయి. ఇక్కడ total potential V ని predict చేయండి — ఒక పెద్ద value, లేదా ఏదైనా surprising?

tts_sentences.text_en: Equal size, equal distance, opposite signs: plus eight point nine and minus eight point nine cancel to exactly zero. That pair adds nothing.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Equal size, equal distance, opposite signs: plus eight point nine और minus eight point nine exactly zero हो जाते हैं। वह pair कुछ नहीं जोड़ता।
tts_sentences.text_te: Equal size, equal distance, opposite signs: plus eight point nine మరియు minus eight point nine exactly zero కి cancel అవుతాయి. ఆ pair ఏమీ కలపదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the total is just charge q₃'s plus twelve point zero. Two charges were present, yet together they contributed nothing.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो total बस charge q₃ का plus twelve point zero है। दो charges present थे, फिर भी मिलकर उन्होंने कुछ नहीं दिया।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి total కేవలం charge q₃ యొక్క plus twelve point zero. రెండు charges present గా ఉన్నాయి, అయినప్పటికీ కలిసి అవి ఏమీ contribute చేయలేదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Two charges sitting nearby must give a large potential, and the signs do not change that.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: An equal +q and −q at equal distance give +8.9 and −8.9, summing to exactly zero — the pair adds nothing while a single q3 gives +12.0.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Signs decide the sum: an equal +q and −q equidistant cancel to zero, no matter how many charges are present.

STATE_5.title: The superpower: one scalar sum vs the field's vector addition

scene_composition.text: potential V: one easy scalar sum of three signed numbers
predict: can we add the field E the same easy way?
no — each E is an arrow; add as vectors, by components first
V = one scalar sum; E = hard vector addition — V is the easier road

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is the superpower. To get the potential V, we just added three signed numbers — one easy scalar sum.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह है superpower। potential V पाने के लिए हमने बस तीन signed numbers जोड़े — एक easy scalar sum।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదే superpower. potential V పొందడానికి మేము కేవలం మూడు signed numbers కలిపాం — ఒక easy scalar sum.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the electric field E at the same point. Predict: can we add the field the same easy way?
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब उसी point पर electric field E। Predict करिए: क्या हम field को उसी easy तरीके से जोड़ सकते हैं?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు అదే point వద్ద electric field E. Predict చేయండి: మనం field ని అదే easy విధంగా కలపగలమా?

tts_sentences.text_en: No. Each charge's field is an arrow pointing a different way. You must add them as vectors, splitting into components first.
tts_sentences.text_hi: नहीं। प्रत्येक charge का field एक arrow है जो अलग दिशा में point करता है। आपको उन्हें vectors की तरह जोड़ना होगा, पहले components में split करके।
tts_sentences.text_te: లేదు. ప్రతి charge యొక్క field వేరే వైపు point చేసే ఒక arrow. మీరు వాటిని vectors గా కలపాలి, ముందు components లోకి split చేసి.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is the whole point: potential is one scalar sum, the field is hard vector addition. Potential is the easier road.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही तो पूरा point है: potential एक scalar sum है, field hard vector addition है। potential आसान रास्ता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే అసలు విషయం: potential ఒక scalar sum, field hard vector addition. potential సులభమైన రహదారి.

STATE_6.title: You drive — drag the probe, slide q₃, watch the sum update

scene_composition.text: drag the probe anywhere — the running sum of three signed terms updates live
slide q₃ to flip or scale it — every term and the total respond at once
ride the centre line to watch the pair cancel; toggle the field to see vectors

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive. Drag the probe anywhere and watch the running sum of the three signed terms update live.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप drive करिए। probe को कहीं भी drag करिए और तीन signed terms के running sum को live update होते देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు drive చేయండి. probe ని ఎక్కడైనా drag చేయండి మరియు మూడు signed terms యొక్క running sum live గా update అవడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Use the slider to flip or scale charge q₃; every signed term and the total respond at once.
tts_sentences.text_hi: charge q₃ को flip या scale करने के लिए slider use करिए; हर signed term और total एक साथ respond करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: charge q₃ ని flip లేదా scale చేయడానికి slider ఉపయోగించండి; ప్రతి signed term మరియు total ఒకే సారి respond చేస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Ride the centre line to watch the positive and negative pair cancel, and toggle the field to see vectors against the simple scalar sum.
tts_sentences.text_hi: positive और negative pair को cancel होते देखने के लिए centre line पर जाइए, और simple scalar sum के खिलाफ vectors देखने के लिए field toggle करिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: positive మరియు negative pair cancel అవడం చూడడానికి centre line పై వెళ్ళండి, మరియు simple scalar sum తో పోల్చి vectors చూడడానికి field toggle చేయండి.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the potential of a system of charges when they can (1) write the total potential as the scalar superposition V = Σ k qᵢ/rᵢ and combine the per-charge contributions by adding signed NUMBERS, not vectors; (2) reject the 'combine like vectors / use directions' belief and treat V as a signed scalar with no components or angles; (3) recognise that every charge contributes a nonzero k qᵢ/rᵢ term — distance only shrinks a term, it never zeroes a far charge out of the sum; (4) reason that an equal +q and −q equidistant from the point cancel exactly to zero, so the count of nearby charges does not by itself make V large — signs and distances decide; (5) contrast the easy scalar potential sum with the FIELD at the same point, which requires vector addition by components; and (6) evaluate a numeric system potential correctly, keeping each term's sign and a 1/r (not 1/r²) falloff.

questions.stem: Three charges q₁, q₂, q₃ sit at fixed positions. At a point P at distances r₁, r₂, r₃ from them, what is the total electric potential V?

options.A: V = k q₁/r₁ + k q₂/r₂ + k q₃/r₃ — add the three signed terms as scalars
options.B: V = k(q₁+q₂+q₃)/(r₁+r₂+r₃) — add the charges and the distances in lumps
options.C: The vector sum of the three k q/r contributions, combined with their directions
options.D: Only k q/r for the nearest charge; the others are too far to count

distractor_misconceptions.B: adds charges and distances in lumps instead of summing each k qᵢ/rᵢ term separately
distractor_misconceptions.C: treats potential as a vector needing directions; V is a scalar, just add the signed numbers
distractor_misconceptions.D: keeps only the nearest charge, but every charge contributes a nonzero k q/r term

questions.tested_idea: The total potential of a system is the scalar sum V = Σ k qᵢ/rᵢ of each charge's own potential.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Several point charges are fixed in space. Write the potential they together produce at a chosen field point, in terms of each charge and its distance.

questions.stem: A point lies exactly equidistant from a +q charge and a −q charge of equal magnitude. What do these two charges together contribute to the potential there?

options.A: Exactly zero — +kq/r and −kq/r cancel: equal size, equal distance, opposite signs
options.B: A large positive value, because two charges are sitting nearby
options.C: Twice kq/r, because the two contributions add up
options.D: kq/r, the value from just one of them

distractor_misconceptions.B: assumes nearby charges must give a big potential, ignoring that opposite signs cancel
distractor_misconceptions.C: adds the magnitudes instead of the signed numbers, missing the opposite signs
distractor_misconceptions.D: keeps one term and drops the other, instead of summing the two opposite signed terms

questions.tested_idea: An equal +q and −q equidistant from a point cancel exactly to zero in the potential sum.
questions.parallel_form_stem: On the perpendicular bisector of a +q and −q pair of equal size, what is the pair's combined contribution to the potential, and why?

questions.stem: The three charges' potentials at a point are +3.4, −6.7, and +11.9 (demo units). How do you combine them to get the total potential?

options.A: Add the signed numbers directly: +3.4 − 6.7 + 11.9 = +8.6 — no directions or components
options.B: Add them as vectors, splitting each into x and y components first
options.D: Keep only the largest, +11.9, since it dominates

distractor_misconceptions.B: treats the scalar potential as a vector needing components; V has no direction
distractor_misconceptions.C: adds magnitudes and drops the signs, but each term keeps its own sign
distractor_misconceptions.D: keeps only the largest term, but every signed term counts in the sum

questions.tested_idea: Potential is a signed scalar: total it by adding the signed numbers directly, no directions.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Three charges contribute potentials of −2, +9, and −4 units at a point. State the total potential and how you combined them.

questions.stem: You move the probe far to the right, so one charge q₁ is now over four units away. How does that distant charge affect the total potential?

options.A: It still adds a small nonzero term k q₁/r₁ — large distance shrinks it but never zeroes it
options.B: It drops out completely; only the nearby charges count
options.C: It contributes just as much as before; distance does not matter for potential
options.D: It flips the sign of the total, because it is now far away

distractor_misconceptions.B: assumes a distant charge contributes nothing, but k q/r is only smaller, never zero
distractor_misconceptions.C: ignores that potential falls with distance as 1/r, so a far charge contributes less
distractor_misconceptions.D: invents a sign flip from distance; distance changes a term's size, not its sign

questions.tested_idea: Every charge contributes a nonzero k q/r term; distance shrinks a far term but never zeroes it.
questions.parallel_form_stem: In a system of charges, one charge is much farther from the field point than the rest. Does it still appear in the potential sum? Explain.

questions.stem: To find the total potential of several charges you just add signed numbers. Why is finding the total electric FIELD at the same point harder?

options.A: The field is a vector — each charge's E points a different way, so you must add them as vectors, by components
options.B: It is not harder; you add the field magnitudes the same scalar way
options.C: The field needs the same scalar sum, just with the signs dropped
options.D: The field cannot be found at all once you know the potential

distractor_misconceptions.B: treats the field like the scalar potential, but the E's add as vectors with directions
distractor_misconceptions.C: imagines a scalar field sum; each E is an arrow pointing a different way
distractor_misconceptions.D: denies the field is computable, but vector superposition (or E = −grad V) gives it

questions.tested_idea: Potential is a scalar sum; the field is a vector sum needing components — that is why V is easier.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Adding the potentials of many charges is one easy sum. Explain why adding their electric fields at the same point takes more work.

questions.stem: Three charges give terms (charge ÷ distance, demo units): +1 at distance 1, −5 at distance 1, and +6 at distance 2. With k = 1, what is the total potential?

distractor_misconceptions.B: adds the magnitudes (1 + 5 + 3) and drops the signs, ignoring that −5 subtracts
distractor_misconceptions.C: keeps only the largest term (+3) instead of summing all three signed terms
distractor_misconceptions.D: uses a 1/r² falloff for the third term, but potential falls as 1/r

questions.tested_idea: Evaluate V = Σ k qᵢ/rᵢ numerically: keep each sign and use a 1/r (not 1/r²) falloff.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Charges give terms +2 at distance 1, −3 at distance 1, and +8 at distance 2 (k = 1). Compute the total potential.

STATE_1.label: Three charges + probe — each gives its own k q / r
STATE_1.formula_overlay: each charge: V = k q / r

STATE_2.label: V is a scalar — add the three signed numbers

STATE_3.label: Push the probe far — the distant charge still counts

STATE_4.label: Equidistant +/− pair cancels — total is just q₃

STATE_5.label: Scalar sum vs the field's vector addition (the aha)
STATE_5.formula_overlay: V: one scalar sum   ·   E: vector sum by components

STATE_6.label: Explore — drag probe, slide q₃, live running sum
STATE_6.formula_overlay: drag the probe — V = Σ k qᵢ/rᵢ (live)
